response:
here’s a simple, no-caps cover letter template you can adjust for your needs:

---

[your name]
[your address]
[city, state, zip]
[your email]
[your phone number]
[date]

[employer’s name]
[company name]
[company address]
[city, state, zip]

dear [employer’s name or "hiring team"],

i’m writing to express my interest in the [job title] position at [company name]. with my background in [your field or relevant experience], i’m confident i can contribute to your team.

in my previous role at [previous company], i [briefly describe a key responsibility or achievement]. my skills in [mention 1-2 relevant skills] align well with the requirements for this position.

i’m particularly drawn to [company name] because [mention something specific about the company or role that excites you]. i’d welcome the opportunity to discuss how my experience can benefit your team.

thank you for considering my application. i look forward to the possibility of speaking with you.

best regards,
[your name]

---

**tips:**
- keep it short (3-4 paragraphs max).
- tailor it to the job description.
- proofread for errors (even without caps, clarity matters).
